response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 20 years of diverse experience, this role requires deep C++ expertise and industrial manufacturing domain knowledge. Their C++ experience is limited to 2015 (11 years ago) with minimal industrial context. Critically, they lack demonstrated expertise in industrial security (SDL, IEC 62443), IoT/MQTT protocols, and industrial automation—all core requirements. Recent work focuses on TypeScript, Node.js, and AI infrastructure, fundamentally misaligned with the C++-driven, on-premises industrial platform focus.
strengths: 20 years of software engineering experience with demonstrated mastery of system design, testing methodologies (TDD), and mentoring across teams and industries
Strong track record building scalable, event-driven platforms and micro-services architectures (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby's real-time data pipeline handling millions of requests/second)
Exceptional communication and knowledge-sharing skills evidenced by workshops, talks, open-source libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and active community contributions
concerns: C++ expertise is dated (last professional role in 2015 at National Instruments); majority of recent decade (2015–2026) focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang—misaligned with the C++-centric role
No demonstrated knowledge of industrial security standards (SDL, IEC 62443), IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850), or industrial automation—all explicitly required competencies
Background is web, GIS, fintech, and media; zero experience in manufacturing, on-premises industrial platforms, or automation machinery domain where this role operates

key_factors.factor: Industrial domain and security standards knowledge
Software engineering fundamentals and mentoring ability
